pi_items.source: Task description (task.json line 5: 'Search for all calendar events where the **Event Name (Title)** contains the word "Review"'). Verified against verifier.py line 57 which hardcodes target_keyword = "Review". Confirmed in setup_data.py which creates 8 events with 'Review' in title: Q2 Financial Review, Product Strategy Review, Marketing Campaign Review, Legal Contract Review, Annual Performance Review - Frank Rivera, HR Policy Review, Quarterly Business Review, Customer Success Review.

Task description (task.json line 5: 'Add the tag "Audit" to each of these events'). Verified against verifier.py line 58 which hardcodes target_tag = "Audit". Confirmed in setup_task.sh (lines 25-26) which searches for calendar.event.type with name='Audit' and removes it before task execution.

Event exists in setup_data.py (lines 128-133) with exact name 'Finance Planning Session' and description 'Monthly finance planning: expense review and budget adjustments.' This event has 'review' in its description but NOT in its title, making it a perfect distractor case. Task explicitly requires checking title only (task.json line 5): 'Do **not** tag events that only mention "review" in the description but have a different title.'

privileged_info_summary: All three metadata claims are verified. The task requires tagging calendar events where the title contains 'Review' with an 'Audit' tag. Eight events with 'Review' in their titles exist in the database: Q2 Financial Review, Product Strategy Review, Marketing Campaign Review, Legal Contract Review, Annual Performance Review - Frank Rivera, HR Policy Review, Quarterly Business Review, and Customer Success Review. The distractor example 'Finance Planning Session' contains 'review' only in its description, not in the title, and must not be tagged per task requirements.
